name: Daedalus
description: Daedalus was a legendary craftsman, inventor, and artist in Greek mythology. He was renowned for his exceptional skill and ingenuity. Born in Athens, he was the son of Metion or Eupalamus, and his ancestry was traced back to the royal house of Erechtheus. Driven by jealousy, Daedalus murdered his nephew Perdix, a talented apprentice, and was forced to flee to Crete. There, he served King Minos and Queen Pasiphae, creating intricate works of art and architecture, including the Labyrinth, a complex maze designed to contain the Minotaur, a creature half-man, half-bull. Daedalus also crafted a wooden cow for Pasiphae to mate with a bull, resulting in the birth of the Minotaur. Imprisoned by Minos to prevent him from revealing the secrets of the Labyrinth, Daedalus, with his son Icarus, fashioned wings of feathers and wax to escape. Despite his warning, Icarus flew too close to the sun, melting the wax and causing him to fall to his death into the sea. Daedalus eventually reached Sicily, where he was welcomed by King Cocalus. Minos pursued him, but Cocalus' daughters killed Minos in his bath. Daedalus is a symbol of human creativity, ingenuity, and the tragic consequences of ambition and hubris. His inventions and crafts are testaments to human potential, while the story of Icarus serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of exceeding limits. He represents the power and peril of innovation and the importance of heeding wise counsel.
subtitle: Master Craftsman and Inventor of Legend
achievements: Built the wooden cow for Pasiphae

keyMyths.title: The Labyrinth
keyMyths.description: Daedalus built the Labyrinth to imprison the Minotaur. It was a complex maze from which escape was nearly impossible, showcasing Daedalus's architectural genius. The Labyrinth became a symbol of intricate design and inescapable traps.

keyMyths.title: The Wings of Icarus
keyMyths.description: To escape Crete, Daedalus crafted wings of feathers and wax for himself and his son, Icarus. He warned Icarus not to fly too close to the sun, but Icarus disobeyed, leading to his tragic downfall. The story is a parable about hubris and the importance of moderation.

keyMyths.title: Death of Minos
keyMyths.description: Pursued by Minos, Daedalus sought refuge in Sicily with King Cocalus. Minos was eventually killed by Cocalus' daughters while taking a bath, marking the end of Minos's reign and Daedalus's escape from his pursuer. This event highlights Daedalus's cunning and resourcefulness.

sources.author: Apollodorus

sources.text: A comprehensive collection of Greek myths and legends.

sources.author: Ovid
